hardware: c8a.4xlarge (AMD Zen5 EPYC 9R45, 16 vCPU) ON-DEMAND, ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo)
dataset: Snowflake arctic-embed-m-v1.5 (MSMARCO v2.1, precomputed, 10M), Matryoshka 768->256, L2-normalized — same prep as 067, independently re-fetched
replication_note: Rerank-C sweep re-ran incidentally on this box: recall matches 067 (us-east-2) to 4 decimals at every C; QPS within ~4%. 067 replicates cross-region.
bound_verdict: COMPUTE-BOUND at 10M: marginal ns/code FALLS from 0.168 (32MB, cache-resident) to 0.151 (320MB, 10x past LLC) — no step up crossing the cache boundary; the per-N average only shrinks as fixed rerank cost amortizes. Tiling confirms: batch=1 is fastest (668 QPS), batch 2-32 all within -10%/-4% of it. A DRAM-bound scan would reward tiling ~linearly; this one doesn't reward it at all.

itq_at_10m.config: ITQ 256x256 learned rotation (50k sample, 30 iters) vs random FWHT, both + residual; nq=1000 (recall differs slightly from the 10k-query sweep), exact GT
itq_at_10m.verdict: ITQ does NOT help at 10M on arctic-256: deltas are noise (+0.002 to -0.002), vs +0.7 to +0.1 pts on 1M OpenAI-256 (066). The 50k training sample is 0.5% of a 10M corpus (vs 5% at 1M); and arctic/MSMARCO differs from OpenAI/dbpedia, so sample-dilution vs embedding-geometry is unresolved.
